channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Market Overview: Canadian and Global Financial Updates Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Global financial markets showed mixed results as investors weighed the potential escalation of conflict in the Middle East and the approaching deadlines for diplomatic agreements. North American marke...

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith: A War Hero Facing Allegations of Murder
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, once celebrated as one of Australia's most decorated soldiers for his bravery in Afghanistan, has been arrested and charged with alleged war crimes, including the murder of Afghan m...

item.title: Philippines Has Enough Fuel Supply Until Mid-May, Says DOE
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) announced that the Philippines has a fuel supply that can last for about 50.42 days, enough coverage until the latter half of May 2026. According to DOE Secretary Sharon...

item.title: Oshoba dominates UK’s Chelsey Arnell with a third-round knockout
item.description: Nigerian boxer Elizabeth Oshoba continued her unbeaten streak with a powerful third-round technical knockout (TKO) against British fighter Chelsey Arnell. The fight, which took place in the super feat...

item.title: Former 'Rich House, Poor House' Participant Details Financial and Personal Ruin
item.description: A former participant on the UK reality show 'Rich House, Poor House' has publicly detailed his dramatic downfall from wealth and success to financial ruin and personal hardship. The individual, who wa...

item.title: Red Volkswagen Beetle Shell Suspended Above Squamish Highway Sparks Safety Concerns
item.description: In Squamish, British Columbia, authorities are urging the public to stay clear of a red Volkswagen Beetle shell that was suspended on a rock face above Highway 99. The shell, reportedly placed as part...

item.title: Two attackers killed in extended gunfight outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul
item.description: On Tuesday, an intense gunfight broke out directly outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul’s financial district, resulting in the deaths of at least two attackers and serious injury to another. The ...

item.title: Defence challenges DNA evidence in 1999 Canberra murder trial
item.description: In the ACT Supreme Court, the defence for Melbourne men Joseph Vekony and Steve Fabriczy has argued that police were overly reliant on DNA evidence in the cold-case murder of Irma Palasics, who was ki...

item.title: Congress Chief Kharge Criticises Assam Government, Predicts Party’s Return to Power
item.description: On April 7, 2026, Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge held a press conference in Guwahati, urging the people of Assam to vote out the BJP-led government, which he described as the most corrupt in th...

item.title: Kenya Records Sharp Jump in Cybersecurity Threats as Digital Usage Grows
item.description: Kenya has witnessed a dramatic spike in cybersecurity threats as the digital economy continues to expand rapidly. According to the Communications Authority of Kenya (CA), cyber threat incidents detect...

item.title: Trump proposes charging ships for Strait of Hormuz after conflict with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump has suggested that after the ongoing conflict with Iran, the United States, claiming itself as the 'winner', could charge vessels for passing through the Strait of Hormuz. This ...

item.title: Kenyan Mother Appeals for Help After Teacher Holds Back Son’s Exam Reward
item.description: A concerned Kenyan mother is appealing for assistance after her son's teacher allegedly withheld KSh 20,000, a reward for his academic achievements. The mother, Sarah, explained that she and her husba...

item.title: Pakistan continues mediation efforts between US and Iran amid rising tensions and Iranian strike on Saudi facilities
item.description: According to two Pakistani sources familiar with the discussions, efforts to facilitate talks between the United States and Iran are still continuing despite escalating military actions. One senior se...

item.title: Morgan Stanley warns RBA likely to pause rate hikes as oil shock slows Australian economy
item.description: Morgan Stanley economists expect the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) to keep interest rates on hold through 2026 before moving to a rate-cutting cycle next year. The investment bank warned that market...

item.title: Hydraulic Oil Spill Forces Subway Closure on Toronto's Line 2
item.description: A hydraulic oil spill has led to the suspension of subway service on a section of Toronto’s Line 2 (Bloor-Danforth) as of early Friday morning. The Toronto Transit Commission (TTC) confirmed the incid...

item.title: UK Passport Fees to Increase from 8 April – Online Applications Offer Savings
item.description: From 8 April 2026, British passport fees are set to rise significantly, marking the third consecutive year of increases. Standard adult passports applied for online will cost £102, up from £94.50, whi...

item.title: Tensions Escalate: Iran Rejects Ceasefire Amid Trump's Threat of Military Action
item.description: Tensions between Iran and the United States have reached a fever pitch as Iran has firmly rejected a ceasefire proposal, with a looming deadline set by President Trump. The situation is volatile, with...

item.title: DICT and Roblox agree on reforms to improve child safety, preventing game ban
item.description: The Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT) announced that there will be no ban on the popular online game Roblox in the Philippines after a meeting with Roblox officials and lo...

item.title: Pakistan Repays $1.43 Billion in Debt, Bolstering Investor Confidence
item.description: According to an official statement released on Tuesday, Pakistan has successfully repaid a significant portion of its external debt, totaling $1.43 billion. This includes a $1.3 billion Eurobond that ...

item.title: RCMP Watchdog's Report on Controversial Unit Delayed Amid Leadership Vacancy
item.description: A long-running investigation into the RCMP's Community-Industry Response Group (C-IRG), tasked with policing protests against resource extraction in British Columbia, has been completed but cannot be ...
